jokes.text: It's me, Bender.
jokes.setup: Dramatic noir narration about a master burglar known only as 'The Cat'
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately reveals his identity in the most casual way possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of noir tropes with Bender's characteristic lack of subtlety. The economy is perfect - three words destroy an entire dramatic setup.

jokes.text: Cock-a-doodle-what-now?
jokes.setup: Bender is startled by sudden daylight
jokes.punchline: He mangles the rooster crow into confused questioning
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that fits Bender's confused criminal persona. Not groundbreaking but well-executed.

jokes.text: Man, what the hell ruined The Cat's pitch-black moonless night?
jokes.setup: Bender just revealed he's 'The Cat' moments ago
jokes.punchline: He refers to himself in third person as if still maintaining the disguise
jokes.explanation: Nice character consistency - Bender's ego makes him cling to the cool identity even after blowing his cover.

jokes.text: And let me add that it is not helping my hangover!
jokes.setup: Professional news report about mysterious cosmic phenomenon
jokes.punchline: Reporter unprofessionally complains about personal hangover
jokes.explanation: Standard 'unprofessional broadcaster' gag. Functional but not particularly inspired.

jokes.text: I know exactly what it is! / I know exactly what it does! / I know exactly why! Oh. No, wait, I don't.
jokes.setup: Professor confidently claims knowledge three times in a row
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he doesn't know the third thing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor Farnsworth moment - his overconfidence building then crashing. The rule of three structure works perfectly.

jokes.text: Well, I think it's because it wants attention.
jokes.setup: Complex scientific discussion about white hole
jokes.punchline: Fry anthropomorphizes cosmic phenomenon with simple psychology
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry logic - reducing cosmic mystery to basic human motivation. Absurd but internally consistent for the character.

jokes.text: Ha, ha! Ha, ha! Ha, ha, ha! Huh... huh... huh...?
jokes.setup: Character apparently laughing at something
jokes.punchline: Laughter transforms into confused grunting
jokes.explanation: Generic confused reaction beat. Without visual context it's hard to evaluate, but seems like standard physical comedy.

jokes.text: I think it means 'attention.'
jokes.setup: Previous confusing signals from white hole
jokes.punchline: Leela matter-of-factly interprets the chaos as confirming Fry's theory
jokes.explanation: Good callback that validates Fry's absurd theory. Leela's deadpan delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Told ya!
jokes.setup: White hole confirms it wants attention
jokes.punchline: Fry smugly claims vindication for his ridiculous theory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - being accidentally right and immediately gloating. Two words maximum efficiency.

jokes.text: Somethin' about my ass?
jokes.setup: Cosmic beings speaking about 'the greatest event that can be witnessed'
jokes.punchline: Bender assumes they're talking about his ass
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender narcissism perfectly timed. His ego makes him think cosmic events revolve around his anatomy.

jokes.text: Look, I'm touched and flattered by the size of this pile
jokes.setup: Professor presents something to convince the President
jokes.punchline: President appreciates a large bribe pile
jokes.explanation: Standard corrupt politician gag. The visual of a literal pile of money is amusing but predictable.

jokes.text: Um, no backsies, but I'm afraid this is one of those rare problems that can't be solved with bribery.
jokes.setup: President just accepted a bribe
jokes.punchline: Immediately says the bribe won't work for this problem
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing the President's shameless corruption. The 'no backsies' adds childish charm.

jokes.text: Bubblegum Tate!
jokes.setup: Secretary of Space being introduced
jokes.punchline: His name is 'Bubblegum Tate'
jokes.explanation: Absurd name for a government official. Simple but memorable character introduction.

jokes.text: Why, if I weren't so old, I'd cider your apples.
jokes.setup: Professor angry at being called old
jokes.punchline: Threatens with nonsensical old-timey phrase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant malapropism that sounds like old-timey fighting words but makes no sense. Perfect Professor Farnsworth confusion.

jokes.text: Mm-hmm. It's the Larry Bird of holes.
jokes.setup: Technical explanation about white hole shooting chronitons
jokes.punchline: Compared to basketball player Larry Bird
jokes.explanation: Unexpected sports metaphor for cosmic phenomenon. The specificity of Larry Bird makes it funnier than generic basketball reference.

jokes.text: Was that a period or a comma? / Ellipses, dig?
jokes.setup: Professor wondering if sentence about dying ended
jokes.punchline: Bubblegum clarifies the punctuation while using slang
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about sentence structure while the character is literally worried about dying mid-sentence.

jokes.text: Ooh, I hate her so much! That should have been me!
jokes.setup: Young girl chosen for space mission
jokes.punchline: Adult Amy jealous of child getting the opportunity
jokes.explanation: Basic jealousy gag. The adult competing with a child angle has some cringe appeal but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: But not as long as the long-lasting taste of Charleston Chew!
jokes.setup: Serious discussion about long space journey
jokes.punchline: Sudden product placement for candy
jokes.explanation: Unexpected product placement parody. The transition from serious to commercial is abrupt and silly.

jokes.text: Gee whiz. By the ship's clock, we had been traveling for 80 years when it all went to hell.
jokes.setup: Sweet young girl starting to tell story
jokes.punchline: Innocent delivery of dark content about everything going wrong
jokes.explanation: Excellent tonal whiplash. The innocent 'gee whiz' followed by dark revelation creates perfect comedic contrast.

jokes.text: Our bodies withered and our lives wasted.
jokes.setup: Cute kids went on space adventure
jokes.punchline: They aged horribly and their lives were ruined
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - the cheerful kids met a horrible fate. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier and more disturbing.

jokes.text: Ooh! Oh yeah. A little to the left.
jokes.setup: Professor asks Bender for something to write on
jokes.punchline: Bender apparently enjoys being written on
jokes.explanation: Standard robot-as-object gag with slight sexual undertone. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: Huh, according to those assulations
jokes.setup: Professor referring to his calculations
jokes.punchline: Makes up word 'assulations' (ass + calculations)
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau that sounds scientific but references Bender's ass. Good Professor Farnsworth technobabble parody.

jokes.text: Aw. I'll take her wallet to remember her by.
jokes.setup: Touching moment about the girl's sacrifice giving meaning to their mission
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately steals from the aged child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - turns sentimental moment into theft opportunity. The fake sentiment makes it funnier.

jokes.text: This Week in the Universe!
jokes.setup: News program title
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'This Week' but expanded to universal scope
jokes.explanation: Simple parody of news show titles. The scale escalation is mildly amusing but not sophisticated.

jokes.text: The stars called and Scruffy said, 'A-yup.'
jokes.setup: Question about Scruffy joining the cosmic mission
jokes.punchline: Deadpan acceptance of universe-spanning adventure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy - treats cosmic destiny with same casual attitude as janitor duties. Character voice is spot-on.

jokes.text: we'll need to be frozen in carbonara
jokes.setup: Scientific explanation of preservation method
jokes.punchline: Confuses carbonite with pasta sauce
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor malapropism. The Star Wars reference twisted into food humor is appropriately silly for the character.

jokes.text: Ooh, I've never been frozen in that before.
jokes.setup: Professor's carbonara mistake
jokes.punchline: Amy treats it as an exciting new experience
jokes.explanation: Mild sexual innuendo that plays into Amy's adventurous character. Not particularly clever but fits the moment.

jokes.text: I doubt there'll be any emergencies. / Emergency!
jokes.setup: Professor confidently states no emergencies expected
jokes.punchline: Immediate emergency announcement
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony setup. Predictable but well-executed timing makes it work despite being a comedy cliché.

jokes.text: Welcome into existence, Scruffy Number One.
jokes.setup: Creating duplicate crew member
jokes.punchline: Formal welcome to artificial existence
jokes.explanation: Sets up the existential absurdity of the duplicates. The formal greeting to artificial life is philosophically funny.

jokes.text: Tell me somethin' I don't feel burnin' in my loins.
jokes.setup: Being told about having all original Scruffy's desires
jokes.punchline: Crude confirmation about sexual urges
jokes.explanation: Pure Scruffy dialogue - crude but delivered with his characteristic matter-of-fact style. Character voice is perfect.

jokes.text: Yeah. I'll get around to it after my break.
jokes.setup: Critical pipe leak needs immediate attention
jokes.punchline: Scruffy prioritizes break time over emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy work ethic - emergency or not, routine comes first. Character consistency is excellent.

jokes.text: You don't get a break. You'll be dead in five minutes.
jokes.setup: Scruffy wanting to take break
jokes.punchline: Casual announcement of impending death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. The contrast between workplace routine and existential horror works well.

jokes.text: Scruffy Number One leaves this world a little better than he found it.
jokes.setup: Scruffy dying after fixing pipe
jokes.punchline: Noble death speech for minor maintenance
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - Scruffy finds dignity in humble work. The heroic tone for basic maintenance is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Scruffy Number One left the lights on.
jokes.setup: Next Fry duplicate created for critical mission
jokes.punchline: Mission is just turning off lights
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd - heroic sacrifice followed by mundane task. The anticlimax works well.

jokes.text: It's been an honor.
jokes.setup: Fry about to die from turning off lights
jokes.punchline: Treats mundane task as heroic duty
jokes.explanation: Continues theme of dignified death for trivial tasks. Fry's earnestness sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: It's hung up on the rim!
jokes.setup: Rocket separation failure
jokes.punchline: Phrasing suggests toilet humor
jokes.explanation: Juvenile double entendre. The toilet humor is fairly lazy though the rocket context gives it some legitimacy.

jokes.text: We're gonna need more cigarettes!
jokes.setup: Crisis situation requiring urgent action
jokes.punchline: Solution is more cigarettes instead of typical resources
jokes.explanation: Subverts 'We're gonna need more X' trope with unexpected item. Mildly amusing but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: I'm a replicant. I don't take orders from a hologram.
jokes.setup: Hologram Professor giving urgent orders
jokes.punchline: Artificial being discriminating against different artificial being
jokes.explanation: Brilliant existential hierarchy joke - artificial beings having prejudice against other artificial beings. Very clever sci-fi comedy.

jokes.text: I'll do it. For him.
jokes.setup: Bender refusing to help hologram
jokes.punchline: Changes mind out of loyalty to original Bender
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing even replica Bender has loyalty to his original. The sentiment is unexpected but earned.

jokes.text: Go Bender! Go Bender, go Bender, go Bender, go Bender! Go Bender, go Bender. Go Bender... Go Bender. Go--
jokes.setup: Crew cheering for Bender as he ages rapidly
jokes.punchline: Chant slows down as they realize he's dying
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy - the enthusiasm deflating as reality sets in. The pacing of the decline is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: That is one dead hero.
jokes.setup: Bender just died heroically
jokes.punchline: Casual, matter-of-fact observation about death
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery that undercuts the heroic moment. Amy's casual tone makes the dark humor work.

jokes.text: My life's work is complete.
jokes.setup: Professor created for some task
jokes.punchline: Considers brief task his life's work
jokes.explanation: Nice existential joke about the brevity of the duplicates' lives and their satisfaction with simple tasks.

jokes.text: No infinite loops on Scruffy Number Four's watch.
jokes.setup: Multiple Scruffy duplicates have been created
jokes.punchline: Current Scruffy takes professional pride in preventing programming errors
jokes.explanation: Good technical humor mixed with Scruffy's work ethic. The programming joke fits the sci-fi setting well.

jokes.text: I can't believe I was brought into existence just to calculate mileage depreciation. But I'm glad I was!
jokes.setup: Hermes complaining about mundane purpose
jokes.punchline: Immediately happy about his boring existence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes - finds joy in bureaucratic tasks others would hate. The quick mood shift is character-perfect.

jokes.text: That's it? My whole existence was just to open a can of sandwich?
jokes.setup: Amy realizing her purpose was opening canned food
jokes.punchline: 'Can of sandwich' instead of 'canned sandwich'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke with existential crisis. The phrasing is awkward in a funny way that fits the absurd situation.

jokes.text: Oh, quit complaining. I keep getting created just to cheer people up before they disintegrate. It works!
jokes.setup: Amy complaining about her trivial purpose
jokes.punchline: Fry's purpose is cheering people up before death, and he's happy about it
jokes.explanation: Dark humor made bright by Fry's optimism. His ability to find joy in being a death cheerleader is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: It's a swarm of space salps. Living creatures attempting to feed on the ship!
jokes.setup: Dangerous space creatures attacking
jokes.punchline: 'Space salps' sounds like made-up sci-fi creature name
jokes.explanation: Decent sci-fi creature naming parody. 'Salps' are real sea creatures, making it funnier for those who know.

jokes.text: I got printed by accident. Is it cool if I stand here and eat some yogurt?
jokes.setup: Battle situation requiring all hands
jokes.punchline: Accidental duplicate just wants to eat yogurt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - casual attitude during crisis. The politeness of asking permission while being useless is endearing.

jokes.text: And all thanks to me, Bender Number Three. I demand a raise!
jokes.setup: Successful completion of mission
jokes.punchline: Temporary duplicate demands raise moments before dying
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender greed even in artificial life. The timing of demanding payment before imminent death is perfect character work.

jokes.text: I'm the greatest!
jokes.setup: Bender about to disintegrate
jokes.punchline: Dies declaring his greatness
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender ego maintained until the very end. Three words that perfectly sum up his character.

jokes.text: That's weird. Where's the yogurt? Oh, here it comes. What a cute little--
jokes.setup: Fry confused about missing yogurt, then seeing something cute
jokes.punchline: Cut off mid-sentence as yogurt creature presumably kills him
jokes.explanation: Good callback with dark payoff. Fry's innocent reaction to the dangerous creature is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You don't care about me? Not even a little.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg asking if anyone cares about him
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest 'not even a little'
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg treatment - the crew's indifference to him is a running theme. The bluntness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I didn't get a kit.
jokes.setup: Told all replicants get customized mission kits
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg didn't get one
jokes.explanation: Continues Zoidberg's exclusion theme. His pathetic observation adds to the character's outsider status.

jokes.text: Such small portions.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg told to eat enormous amount of yogurt
jokes.punchline: Complains portions are too small
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg gluttony. Complaining about 'small portions' of what's clearly massive amounts fits his character.

jokes.text: I've got a stomach and a mouth, what else does a guy need?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg not fully printed yet
jokes.punchline: Reduces existence to basic eating functions
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg philosophy - life simplified to consumption. The existential reduction is appropriately crude for him.

jokes.text: That was 600 pounds of yogurt! No wonder I'm still hungry.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg finished eating the yogurt
jokes.punchline: 600 pounds wasn't enough to satisfy him
jokes.explanation: Typical Zoidberg appetite escalation. The casual complaint about 600 pounds being insufficient is absurd.

jokes.text: Right here. Where are you? Right here. Oops.
jokes.setup: Hermes looking for Zoidberg with weapon
jokes.punchline: They're in same place, Hermes accidentally shoots him
jokes.explanation: Basic slapstick misunderstanding. The 'oops' sells it but it's fairly standard physical comedy.

jokes.text: Feh, too spicy.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg just shot/hurt
jokes.punchline: Complains about spiciness instead of injury
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - instead of pain reaction, Zoidberg critiques the flavor. Perfect character prioritization.

jokes.text: Wait, disposable?
jokes.setup: Professor calling them disposable maintenance copies
jokes.punchline: Just now realizing they're considered disposable
jokes.explanation: Good existential horror beat. The delayed realization of their expendable nature adds dark humor.

jokes.text: The Whuttle Wubble?
jokes.setup: Professor mentions 'Shuttle Bubble'
jokes.punchline: Confused mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation gag. The alliteration is mildly amusing but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: The hell with them! Why should they get all the credit after my Frycestors slaved and shirked for millions of years?!
jokes.setup: Fry realizing the duplicates did all the work
jokes.punchline: 'Frycestors' - ancestors but with his name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant portmanteau that's both clever wordplay and character-specific. 'Frycestors' is instantly memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Live fast and die old, that's what I always say.
jokes.setup: Crew aging rapidly due to time effects
jokes.punchline: Reverses 'live fast die young' saying to fit situation
jokes.explanation: Good situational reversal of common saying. The twist fits their aging predicament perfectly.

jokes.text: Before such beauty, my ass weeps.
jokes.setup: Witnessing birth of new universe
jokes.punchline: Bender's ass has emotional reaction to beauty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that transforms crude body humor into poetic absurdity. Peak Bender philosophy.

jokes.text: We are? / I'm back, baby?
jokes.setup: Told they're already back from mission
jokes.punchline: Confused about completing mission they never experienced
jokes.explanation: Good ironic ending setup. Their confusion about missing their own adventure sets up the final revelation.

jokes.text: We have no idea what happened because we never even woke up. / Shut up and take your medals!
jokes.setup: Leela explaining they missed everything
jokes.punchline: President doesn't care, gives medals anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending irony - the real heroes get credit for doing nothing while duplicates who did everything get nothing. The President's dismissal is perfectly bureaucratic.
